current_role: OpinionStanceFrame-internal unresolved existential-domain or copular-complement omission
observed_overlap: Both preserve visible predicate material and require discourse for missing content.
decisive_difference: Current constructor is limited to `有/冇` and `係/唔係` stance complements; it does not cover ordinary transitive predicates with null NP objects.

observed_overlap: A question or prior discourse can supply unpronounced material.
decisive_difference: A null-object clause can retain subject and predicate and occur outside an answer turn; a fragment may omit most of a proposition.

observed_overlap: A discourse topic may antecede an unpronounced argument.
decisive_difference: The antecedent can be outside the parsed span, and TopicComment does not independently establish predicate valency or license a gap.

observed_overlap: Shares the surface absence of an overt object.
decisive_difference: Token count cannot distinguish lexical one-place valency, optional objects, and discourse-recoverable null objects.

current_role: Verb/use-specific allowance for an objectless surface
observed_overlap: May produce the same subject-plus-predicate string.
decisive_difference: Optionality and anaphoric recovery are distinct lexical/discourse claims and require sense-specific evidence.

current_role: Selectively licensed omission of verbal projection
observed_overlap: May leave only a modal, aspectual, or auxiliary licensor overt.
decisive_difference: Omits predicate-level material rather than one NP argument; Lee and Pan document licensor-specific contrasts.

current_role: Contextually interpreted nominal without an overt lexical head
observed_overlap: Shares discourse recovery and an unpronounced nominal description.
decisive_difference: Occupies an overt nominal constituent and does not establish an unpronounced argument position selected by a predicate.

current_role: Theoretical account connecting null arguments to discourse topics
observed_overlap: Can model an antecedent-dependent unpronounced NP.
decisive_difference: Checked sources use competing null-topic, null-pronoun, argument-ellipsis, and NP-ellipsis analyses; no unique representation is established.
